    <h1 {...{
      "id": "install-a-uxp-plugin"
    }}>{`Install a UXP plugin`}</h1>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "important-note"
    }}>{`Important Note!`}</h2>
    <p>{`Before you can connect to a UXP plugin running within Premiere Pro, you must enable 'Developer Mode', in Premiere Pro's Plugins preferences.`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP plugins are commonly installed directly by the Creative Cloud Desktop application.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The installation options are slightly different depending on the distribution channel you are using—i.e., whether you are installing a plugin directly from the Creative Cloud Marketplace, a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` installer file obtained from independent channels, or deploying it in an enterprise environment.`}</p>

    <p>{`Once the installation is complete, the plugin will be available in the Plugins section of the Creative Cloud Desktop application application, as well as in the host application. For Premiere, it will be the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Plugins`}</strong>{` menu.`}</p>

    <p>{`If the installation fails, click the Details link on the red toast to open the Logs and check the error message. See `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#troubleshooting"
      }}>{`Troubleshooting`}</a>{` for more details.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "uninstall-a-plugin"
    }}>{`Uninstall a Plugin`}</h2>
    <p>{`In the Creative Cloud Desktop application, "Manage Plugins" tab, right-click on the `}<strong parentName="p">{`•••`}</strong>{` icon within a plugin card and select `}<strong parentName="p">{`Uninstall`}</strong>{` to delete it. There's also the option to `}<strong parentName="p">{`Disable`}</strong>{` the plugin, which will remove it from the host application but keep it installed in the Creative Cloud Desktop application. Both actions will pop a confirmation dialog.`}</p>

          "marginRight": "auto",

        }
      }}>{`
      `}<span parentName="span" {...{
          "className": "gatsby-resp-image-background-image",
          "style": {
            "paddingBottom": "51.87500000000001%",
            "position": "relative",
            "bottom": "0",

          }
        }}></span>{`

    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`As shown in the screenshot above, there’s a `}<strong parentName="p">{`distinction between plugins installed from the Creative Cloud Marketplace`}</strong>{` (left) `}<strong parentName="p">{`and those installed from a `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` file`}</strong>{` (right, indicated by the orange download icon).`}</p>
    <p>{`When you uninstall a plugin that was installed from the Marketplace, it's removed from the host application but `}<strong parentName="p">{`remains visible`}</strong>{` in the Manage Plugins tab. This is because you still have the right to reinstall it at any time. In contrast, plugins installed from a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` file `}<strong parentName="p">{`disappear entirely`}</strong>{` from the Manage Plugins tab once uninstalled.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "use-the-upia-tool"
    }}>{`Use the UPIA tool`}</h2>
    <p>{`Under the hood, the Creative Cloud Desktop application uses a command-line utility called the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Unified Plugin Installer Agent`}</strong>{` (UPIA) to manage UXP plugins. Please refer to the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://helpx.adobe.com/creative-cloud/apps/integration-with-other-apps/manage-plugins/install-plugins-using-upia-tool.html"
      }}>{`UPIA tool documentation`}</a>{` for a detailed usage guide for both macOS and Windows.`}</p>
    <p>{`The UPIA tool can `}<strong parentName="p">{`install`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`uninstall`}</strong>{`, and `}<strong parentName="p">{`list`}</strong>{` plugins. Here's a quick summary of the available commands.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "on-macos"
    }}>{`On macOS`}</h3>
    <p>{`Open the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Terminal`}</strong>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`cd`}</inlineCode>{` to the UPIA executable folder—admin privileges may be required:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`cd "/Library/Application Support/Adobe/Adobe Desktop Common/RemoteComponents/UPI/UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent/UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.app/Contents/macOS"

./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --help
./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --version
./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --install <extension-file-path>
./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --remove <extension-file-path>
./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --list <all || product display name>
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`A few examples:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --install "~/Desktop/Test-xjluvc_premierepro.ccx"
./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --remove "startup-test"
./UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent --list all
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "on-windows"
    }}>{`On Windows`}</h3>
    <p>{`Open the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Command Prompt`}</strong>{`, type this command and press Enter:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`cd "C:\\Program Files\\Common Files\\Adobe\\Adobe Desktop Common\\RemoteComponents\\UPI\\UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent"
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Here's a list of the available commands—admin privileges may be required:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /help
UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /version
UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /install <extension-file-path>
UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /remove <extension-file-path>
UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /list <all || product display name>
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`A few examples:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /install "C:\\Temp\\Test-xjluvc_premierepro.ccx"
UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /remove "startup-test"
UnifiedPluginInstallerAgent.exe /list all
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "troubleshooting"
    }}>{`Troubleshooting`}</h2>
    <p>{`While UXP plugins are generally very reliable in terms of management, occasional issues may arise.`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`It is helpful to `}<strong parentName="li">{`run the host application at least once`}</strong>{` before installing any plugin.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`If the Creative Cloud Desktop application is not launching when you double-click on a `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` file, try locating the UPIA executable and running it manually. The `}<strong parentName="li">{`file extension may not have been associated properly`}</strong>{`, or there might be `}<strong parentName="li">{`permission issues`}</strong>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`It may happen that the UPIA executable is either corrupted or not present in the system. `}<strong parentName="li">{`Reinstalling the Creative Cloud Desktop application`}</strong>{` may resolve the issue. If you are installing plugins in an enterprise environment, please `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../enterprise-distribution/index.md#2-bundle-ccx-plugins-in-managed-packages"
        }}>{`check here`}</a>{` how to include the UPIA in your package.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Try using `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://install.anastasiy.com/"
        }}>{`Anastasiy's Extension Manager`}</a>{` (third-party tool) to install the plugin.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Reach out to `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "mailto:ccintrev@adobe.com"
        }}>{`ccintrev@adobe.com`}</a>{` for support. If further troubleshooting is necessary, you may be asked to run the `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://helpx.adobe.com/creative-cloud/apps/troubleshoot/diagnostics-repair-tools/run-log-collector-tool.html"
        }}>{`Log Collector tool`}</a>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
